what_it_does: A localhost-only HTTP gateway that exposes the same FastMCP tool registry used by the stdio face. It authenticates callers via a local dev token or Supabase JWT, enforces an identity-tier tool filter based on each tool's declared posture, writes a mandatory audit record before dispatch, and proxies JSON-RPC initialize/tools/list/tools/call requests to the registered tool functions.
observations: Reuses _face.build_mcp() and _face.mcp._tool_manager instead of building a second engine.
Access is identity-derived: operator (dev token or matching Supabase sub) sees all tools; any other valid user sees only tools tagged posture=='safe'.
Unclassified tools default to operator-only because _tool_posture returns ''.
Mandatory audit posts to a Supabase REST table when configured and raises on failure; a local jsonl mirror is best-effort.
The dev-token bypass is hard-disabled when REMOTE_PUBLIC is set.
GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource returns RFC 8707 metadata without authentication.
GET /mcp currently returns 405 because SSE streaming is not implemented.
All tool dispatch is serialized under a single DISPATCH_LOCK.
The server binds to 127.0.0.1 only, default port 8772.
standouts: Live-registry access control derived from tool posture rather than a hand-maintained allow-list.
Audit-write failure is treated as a call failure (fail-loud).
Local dev-token bypass is explicitly disabled for public-facing instances.
Direct reuse of the stdio face's tool manager and tool functions.
conventions: Fail-closed defaults for missing tokens, unclassified tools, and missing JWT configuration.
Extensive architectural comments in the module docstring and inline.
concerns: If SUPABASE_AUDIT_REST_URL/SUPABASE_AUDIT_KEY are unset, _audit returns silently even if the jsonl mirror failed, so an unaudited call could succeed.
The global DISPATCH_LOCK serializes every tool call, which is simple but not concurrent.
PUBLIC_AS_URL derivation from SUPABASE_JWKS_URL is string-based and could break with unexpected URL shapes.
JWKS algorithm fallback to ES256 when algorithm_name is missing may not match the actual key algorithm.
No Content-Type validation on incoming POST bodies.
Audit args_preview may capture sensitive argument values.
notes: A security-oriented local remote-MCP inlet that reuses the existing stdio tool registry and wraps it with Supabase JWT auth, posture-based tiering, and mandatory audit.
questions: How is the posture tag actually attached to each tool object in the registry?
What is the exact type and attribute layout of objects in _TOOL_MANAGER._tools?
What happens if _face.build_mcp() is invoked more than once?
Is the SSE GET /mcp stream intended to be implemented later, or will it remain unsupported?
How is the connector_audit table protected against unauthorized inserts?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is transport + auth + posture-filter + audit while reusing the stdio engine; the file implements that, with the tool-dispatch proxy already present and SSE GET left as a stub.
intention_signals: To give a Claude Design remote connector a safe HTTP inlet to the existing MCP tool surface without duplicating the engine, using identity and tool posture to enforce least-privilege exposure and audit every call.
novelty: Merging a live FastMCP tool manager with external Supabase JWT validation, posture-derived tiered visibility, and mandatory audit-as-a-gate in a single small HTTP handler.
contribution: A remote MCP HTTP handler that centralizes authentication, tiered authorization via tool posture, mandatory audit logging, and JSON-RPC dispatch over the existing stdio face tool registry.
summary_for_embedding: company/mcp_face/remote.py is a localhost-only remote MCP HTTP gateway that reuses the stdio face's FastMCP tool registry, authenticates via local dev token or Supabase JWT, enforces identity-tier tool visibility based on tool posture, logs every call to a mandatory audit sink, and dispatches JSON-RPC tool calls.
intention_for_embedding: To expose the existing stdio MCP tool surface over HTTP to a remote connector while adding transport, identity-based access control, posture filtering, and mandatory audit without duplicating the engine.

symbols.does: Reads a registered tool's declared posture tag.
symbols.description: Inspects the tool object for a posture attribute or annotation and returns its string value; an empty result means the tool is unclassified and therefore operator-only under the fail-closed client tier.

symbols.does: Filters the live tool registry by caller tier.
symbols.description: Returns sorted name/object pairs from _TOOL_MANAGER._tools: the operator tier receives all tools, the client tier receives only tools whose posture equals 'safe', and any other tier receives nothing.

symbols.does: Writes a mandatory audit record for every remote call.
symbols.description: Serializes call metadata to a local jsonl mirror best-effort and, when configured, posts the row to the Supabase connector_audit table via REST; raises RuntimeError if the table insert fails so an unaudited call cannot succeed.

symbols.does: Produces a compact JSON preview of call arguments.
symbols.description: Serializes the supplied arguments dict to JSON, falling back to str, and returns the first n characters to keep audit rows compact.

symbols.does: Validates a Supabase JWT and derives the caller tier.
symbols.description: Decodes the token using either a symmetric HS256 secret or an asymmetric JWKS key, requires exp and sub, checks the audience, then maps the subject to operator if it matches OPERATOR_USER_ID or client otherwise; returns an ok flag, subject, tier, and reason.
Lazily constructs and caches a jwt.PyJWKClient for SUPABASE_JWKS_URL so JWKS key resolution and kid matching are reused across requests.

symbols.does: Selects between local dev-token and Supabase JWT authentication.
symbols.description: Extracts the Bearer token; if it equals COMPANY_REMOTE_DEV_TOKEN and the instance is not public it grants operator tier, otherwise delegates to Supabase JWT validation; fails closed on any invalid or missing token.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler for the remote MCP endpoint.
symbols.description: Subclass of BaseHTTPRequestHandler that serves OAuth resource metadata and health checks unauthenticated, validates Bearer tokens for all other routes, and implements JSON-RPC initialize, tools/list, and tools/call over /mcp.
Sets the status code, Content-Type and Content-Length headers, ends headers, and writes the provided byte body.
Serializes a dict to JSON and delegates to _send with an application/json content type.

Returns RFC 8707 OAuth protected-resource metadata at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource, a health check at /healthz, rejects unauthenticated requests for other paths, and returns 405 for /mcp SSE streams.

Authenticates the request, parses JSON-RPC, handles initialize and notifications/initialized, returns the tier-filtered tool list for tools/list, and for tools/call enforces tier access, writes mandatory audit, dispatches the registered tool function under DISPATCH_LOCK, and returns text content or error results.
Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to prefix a remote-mcp tag and write to standard error.
Parses an optional port argument, binds ThreadingHTTPServer to 127.0.0.1 on that port, prints a startup banner with registry size and policy summary, and runs until interrupted.
